title: no curl from inside container
body:
/kind bug

curl inside a container fails.
- only to a few sites.
- the site fails from every container (indepent this construction: ubi-based, centos8/9 based, debian-based, nextcloud, no matter)
- podman 4.2 with ipv6 network
- Other sites, ipv4 or ipv6, http/https works fine.
- curl from command line outside container works well ( in my case: fedora core os, stable branch)
-This is most annoying with the original nextcloud container, no updates are possible.

A Example from original nextcloud-image container:
```
curl  -vvv https://objects.githubusercontent.com
* Rebuilt URL to: https://objects.githubusercontent.com/
*   Trying 185.199.110.133...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to objects.githubusercontent.com (185.199.110.133) port 443 (#0)
* ALPN, offering h2
* ALPN, offering http/1.1
* successfully set certificate verify locations:
*   CAfile: /etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt
  CApath: none
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
[stopped and wait until timeout]
```

```
# podman network inspect pp-net 
[
     {
          "name": "pp-net",
          "id": "b654af71438a10b49aa80930fff3e24a35f56851287e498eb684819a2dd2dc1b",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2022-09-21T00:52:03.631572855+02:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               },
               {
                    "subnet": "fd35:5581:6765:7b04::/64",
                    "gateway": "fd35:5581:6765:7b04::1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": true,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
```

```
podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 16:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Do you have any special routing rules or a VPN setup? Please provide `podman info` output as requested in the issue template.
Does it only fail when ipv6 is enabled? From your example it looks like it doesn't reach the ipv4 address.

indeed, its routed through a wireguard vpn.

both with and without --ipv6 same issue

ok, thx @Luap99 its a problem with vpn wireguard.

if the vpn is stopped the routing is working.

unfortunately i cant determine exactly when this problem appears. any time in the last month.
I set container network mtu to 1420. that's fix this problem

I think you stated the problem is solved, anyways can't be solved by Podman as I understand it so closing.
Continue conversation here is fine.
